messages.content: You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Profile the consortium partners, explain complementarity, and justify the partnership composition.
Write the Consortium as a whole section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

3.3 Using feedstocks generated with practices contributing to climate change mitigation and/or adaptation: We will improve climate-related impacts as described in section 1.2.1. KPI 4 - Improve environmental sustainability of bio-based production processes and value chains. 4.1 Innovative & sustainable processes that contribute to GHG emission reduction: i) Our product carbon footprint will be reduced by 100% as shown in section 1.2.5; ii) Reduction in GHG by green energy production (photovoltaics and biomass boiler); iii) Energy-efficient systems implemented in our new factory (sec 1.2.3.1 (1)). Page 37 of 70 4.2 Developing innovative & sustainable processes that improve resource efficiency and zero waste: i) Use of industrial side streams as feedstocks (upcycling); ii) Developing a dynamic analysis cascade approach with 12 valorisation routes for the complete utilisation of all feedstocks ensuring zero waste. 4.3 Developing innovative & sustainable processes enabling us to address zero pollution: Specific actions will be implemented to reduce pollution: i) solid waste; ii) air; iii) water; iv) soil and v) noise (see sec 1.2.3.1). 4.4 Innovative & sustainable processes with improved energy efficiency: Energy efficiency by design has been considered in our proposal (sec 1.2.3.1 (1)). 4.5 Improving life cycle environmental performance: Products obtained in our new biorefinery will present a reduction of 100% carbon footprint according to LCA. KPI 5 - Expand circularity in bio-based value chains. 5.2 Developing circular production practices (including industrial & industrial urban symbiosis): i) 3 of the feedstocks used in our biorefinery are underexploited side streams from other industries; ii) Side streams produced in our project will be further valorised as biobased fertilizers and/or food/feed ingredients and bioenergy. KPI 7 - Improve the market uptake of bio-based products - Brand owners involved as project partners and/or engaged with other mechanisms: i) 6 brand owners from target sectors (nutraceutical, food, feed, cosmetic) are partners in our project; ii) Our multi-actor approach will ensure to consider additional stakeholders in the project, including additional brand owners out of our consortium. c) Requirements and potential barriers FINANCIAL BARRIER: Investment needed to co-fund the Flagship. SOLUTION: As explained in the BP, we, as the coordinator of the proposal and responsible for flagship co-financing, have a totally solid financial situation which will warrant the resources for the factory. BARRIER: High current assets to cover operations until our SUSTAINEXT products reach the market. SOLUTION: A combination of ready-to-market and medium time-to-market products will reduce cash-flow tensions. Moreover, we, as the coordinator, have a solid financial position to cover the needed circulant cash flow. PRODUCTION BARRIER: Our flagship will need to process new feedstocks. SOLUTION: We will design a versatile and adaptable configuration that will be able to extract a wide variety of plant-based phytochemicals from different feedstocks. BARRIER: Future water limitations derived from climate change/water shortages may limit our operations in the factory. SOLUTION: Our current processes have been designed with an 80% water recovery/reuse, minimizing the risk of water scarcity in the factory. MARKET UPTAKE BARRIER: Current market demand foreseen in the BP may differ from future real demands. The market may demand different products from the ones foreseen in the BP. SOLUTION: DYANA is a dynamic system which can optimize our factory performance to meet real market demands. Besides, we will deliver 46 plant-based healthy extracts and functional ingredients, representing a very diversified model and assuring market feasibility. BARRIER: Innovative products and formulations may require scientific efficacy support in order to enter the market. SOLUTION: End users within our project will perform the necessary validation testing to be ready to be marketed at the end of the project. BARRIER: Extracts and ingredients with different innovation degrees may require a different time to market. SOLUTION: We will deliver 46 plant-based healthy extracts and functional ingredients: 21 are commodities that will enter fast into the market (also considering our SUSTAINEXT USP: cost, quality/traceability, sustainability). 12 are newly produced in Europe (imported from APAC), but with moderate presence in the European market, which will enter the market gradually. The last 13 are totally new on the market and will require additional marketing effort but will allow us to differentiate from the competition. This strategy warrants the business opportunity of our proposed solution. BARRIER: Current herbal extract and bioproduct competition - Competitive cost/prices of the final products. SOLUTION: We will beat the competition by producing cost-effective ingredients, with quality/traceability guaranteed and more sustainable (neutral carbon footprint). REGULATORY BARRIER: Commercialization of certain bioproducts may need previous authorization processes by the European Food Safety Agency (EFSA) or the European Medicine Agency (EMA), which are long and expensive. Page 38 of 70 SOLUTION: To guarantee the economic feasibility of our SUSTAINEXT from the very beginning, product commercialization will be gradual, and it will start by the industries where regulatory status is already approved such as food supplements, cosmetics, pharmaceutical, and chemical industries. BARRIER: Regulatory limitations to produce energy with biomass coming from hydroalcoholic extraction which can be considered a waste and require additional licenses. SOLUTION: Our new desolventization unit will improve alcohol recovery to 99.5%, ensuring. If needed, we will conduct the necessary licensing process. BARRIER: Process patents can be weak and sometimes self-defeating considering the detailed explanation given in patent description. SOLUTION: An in-depth patenting analysis will be conducted throughout our project to evaluate the risk-benefit assessment of patenting. Secret know-how will also be evaluated. SOCIAL BARRIERS BARRIER: Tobacco farmers are a very conservative sector and can be reluctant to change into new crops. SOLUTION: We will showcase and demonstrate a lucrative business case which will serve as a practical example for future farmers. Moreover, TABACOEX, as a cooperative that encompasses many farmers, will also play an important role in moving farmers to new health-promoting crops. BARRIER: Hervas and the local community may be reluctant to the installation of an industry in a region whose main industry is sustainable and ecotourism. SOLUTION: The Materiality assessment already conducted, together with our multi-actor approach methodology, will assure to align community priorities with industrial development. Besides, the factory that is already installed has demonstrated a positive impact for the community and our new flagship will showcase an example of environmental responsibility, totally aligned with community priorities. Business case and business model The worldwide plant-based extracts market has been valued at 23.7 billion in 2019 and is expected to reach $59.4 billion by 2025, growing at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 16.5% for the 2019-2025 period. A total of 4 main market segments are targeted through our project, consisting of the food supplements, food ingredients, pet and feed additives, cosmetics, and performance chemicals verticals. A complete business plan is included as an annex. In regard to the impact of our SUSTAINEXT-derived turnover to the overall revenue generation of NATAC, we estimate it will enable the company to step up its leadership positioning within the plant-based extracts and to set a foothold in emerging markets, such as APAC, while strengthening operations in Europe and North America. Our SUSTAINEXT entry into operation is expected by mid-2027, while the first sales are expected by the beginning of 2028. All in all, we expect the extracts revenue stream to grow from 19.08M€ in 2022 to more than 194.71M€ by 2032, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of +26.15% for the 10-year forecasted period. In parallel, costs will be greatly improved which will further impact our net profit. Net Profit is set to increase at a sustained positive rate to reach 94.11M€ by the end of the forecasted period, as scale-up is achieved, and market demand grows. In parallel, our Net Profit Margin will be greatly improved scaling up from 17.58% as of 2022 to 48.33% by 2032, as a result of an economy of scale and a more integrated and efficient value chain. For the foreseen 10-year forecasted period (2022-2032), our SUSTAINEXT project is estimated to deliver positive profitability performance. The Net Present Value (NPV) for the 10-Year forecasted period has been estimated at 271.33 million €, considering a Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC) of 7.04% (current NATAC’s value as of 2022), and a positive Internal Rate of Return (IRR) of 64.59%. Our project’s expected Return on Investment (ROI) is 26.67. The evolution of revenue, EBITDA, and net profit is shown in figure 2.2. Figure 2.2 Revenue, EBITDA, and net margin evolution. Page 39 of 70 End users’ business case: - STAR (food): In collaboration with our SUSTAINEXT project, Star plans to diversify its portfolio and strengthen its functional food line. The extracts that we will provide to the market will be used to create a children’s tea. In this way, the company expects to

### Social sciences and humanities (SSH):  
Our consortium recognizes the critical role of social sciences and humanities (SSH) expertise in addressing user behavior and adoption barriers related to our innovative biorefinery processes. By engaging with SSH researchers, we will explore public perceptions of bio-based products, identify potential resistance to adopting new technologies, and understand the socio-economic impacts of our solutions. This interdisciplinary approach will enable us to design policies that facilitate smoother transitions to sustainable practices, ensuring that our technological advancements are not only environmentally sound but also socially acceptable and beneficial.

### Open science and gender aspects of R&I:  
We are committed to open science practices, ensuring that our research outputs, including data and software, are shared openly and adhere to FAIR principles. Our consortium will implement open access policies to enhance the visibility and accessibility of our findings. In terms of gender aspects, we strive for gender balance within our team and will develop a Gender Equality Plan (GEP) to promote equal opportunities and address gender biases in research design. By considering gender dimensions in our project, we aim to ensure that our innovations are inclusive and equitable, ultimately benefiting a diverse range of stakeholders.
